data.body: AC#2 execution plan (mechanism verified via code trace): SAFE path = drift-class-A TERMINAL_TRANSITION (NOT the GC/delete path, which never deletes a Jira issue anyway — RETIRE only soft-deletes a binding on an already-404 issue). Steps: (1) create a tagged synthetic OPEN ticket; (2) bridge syncs -> REB issue+binding; (3) bilateral pre-heal backup (REST issue-JSON export + git tag on tickets branch, abort-if-either-fails); (4) rebar archive the local -> off-snapshot; (5) manual --mode dry-run PREVIEW (read-only, cap 0) captures the census: exactly 1 terminal_transition, breaker allowed (1/~854<<0.10 = capped); (6) let the bridge APPLY the heal (verify REB issue -> Done via REST before/after) — ZERO manual live passes, the bridge does all live mutations; (7) guaranteed cleanup: delete the REB test issue + purge the local ticket/binding; (8) document artifacts+evidence. Non-destructive; the synthetic issue is my own throwaway.
